input.background: Exercise 19.1 considered whether participation in a follow-up survey was associated with having medical aid. Race was a confounding variable in this analysis. Table 19.5 lists both crude and race-specific cross tabulations.
input.data:

\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}

input.question: Hypothesis test

output.answer: A. Hypotheses. $H_0: R R_1=R R_2$ (no interaction) versus $H_2: R R_1 \neq R R_2$ (interaction) B. Test statistic. $X^2_{\text{stat,int}}=0.005$ with $df=1$ C. $P$-value. $P=0.944$. There is no significant difference in the strata-specific $RR$ estimates (retain $H_0$ of no statistical interaction).

Follow-up_All.description: Counts of subjects who participated or did not participate in the follow-up survey across all subjects, categorized by having medical aid or not.

Medical_Aid_All.description: Medical aid status (having health insurance or not) for all subjects.

Race.description: Race of the subjects, used as a confounding variable in the analysis.

Follow-up_Whites.description: Counts of white subjects who participated or did not participate in the follow-up survey, categorized by having medical aid or not.

Medical_Aid_Whites.description: Medical aid status (having health insurance or not) for white subjects.

Follow-up_Blacks.description: Counts of black subjects who participated or did not participate in the follow-up survey, categorized by having medical aid or not.

Medical_Aid_Blacks.description: Medical aid status (having health insurance or not) for black subjects.

meta_info.caption: Is participating in a follow-up survey associated with having medical aid?
